summary: A symmetrical fortress-like structure with a central rectangular main building featuring a gable roof, flanked by four tall corner towers with flat tops. The building uses sandstone/clay walls with brick roof, white stone foundation blocks, and regular window openings. The design emphasizes vertical elements and defensive aesthetics.
rebuild_hints: Build four tall corner towers (approximately 3 blocks wide, 10+ blocks tall) with flat tops and overhanging caps
Connect towers with walls forming a rectangle, approximately 16 blocks wide by 14 blocks deep
Create central gable roof structure rising above the connecting walls
Place regular window openings in a grid pattern on all walls (approximately 2 blocks wide, 1 block tall)
Add white stone foundation blocks around the perimeter base
Use brick material for the gable roof with wood trim
Ensure symmetry along both north-south and east-west axes
Add wooden overhangs/caps to the top of each tower
uncertainties: Exact block count for tower height uncertain; estimate 10-12 blocks based on proportions
Interior layout not visible from exterior views
Precise window spacing pattern difficult to determine; appears regular but exact offset unclear
Material distinction between sandstone and clay uncertain; appears to be tan/beige colored block
Roof material appears to be brick but could be terracotta or similar
Whether towers are solid or hollow internally cannot be determined
